body:value: Ever married women or men are persons who have been married at least once in their lives although their current marital status may not be married. Currently married women or men are persons who have been married and are not either divorced, widowed or separated. Persons living in consensual unions or in visiting partnerships are also included in the currently married category. It include details on ever married and currently married population by age at marriage, duration of marriage and religious community.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
State
field_group_name:name: Ever married and currently married population
field_ministry_department:name: Ministry of Home Affairs
Department of Home
Registrar General and Census Commissioner, India
field_sector:name: Census and Surveys
Census
Statistics
title: Ever married and currently married population by age at marriage, duration of marriage and religious community - India and States

body:value: Ever married women or men are persons who have been married at least once in their lives although their current marital status may not be married. Currently married women or men are persons who have been married and are not either divorced, widowed or separated. Persons living in consensual unions or in visiting partnerships are also included in the currently married category. It includes details on ever married and currently married population by age at marriage, duration of marriage and educational level.

body:value: Data on residential and cumulative Dwelling and number Projects Approved by Housing and Urban Development Corporation Limited (HUDCO) has been furnished. Housing & Urban Development Corporation Ltd, a premier techno-financial institution was set up by the government of India to facilitate Housing & Infrastructure development in the country with a social motto of serving the unserved and thus bridging the housing divide in the country.

Development

body:value: Railway Density of India has been furnished in the data. Railway density is is measured as train kilometres per running track.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Indian Railways
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Transport
Railways
title: Railway Density of India

body:value: Length of Railways has been furnished in the data. Indian Railways have a history and rich heritage spanning over 150 years. India Railways is a state owned public utility of Government of India under the Ministry of Railways. As a national common carrier transporting passenger and goods over vast network, it has played a key role in Indias social and economic development. It is a cheap and affordable means of transportation for millions of passengers. As a carrier of bulk freight namely ores and minerals, iron and steel, Cement, mineral oils, food grains and fertilizers etc, the importance of Indian Railways for development of Agriculture, Industry and for the benefit of common men is well recognized.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Indian Railways
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Transport
Railways
title: Length of Railways in India

body:value: Get Railway Traffic and Earnings data of India. Data provides Railway Traffic and Earnings from Goods Traffic Originating, Goods Carried, Passengers Originating and Passengers Kilometres etc.

field_group_name:name: Indian Railways
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Transport
Railways
title: Railway Traffic and Earnings

body:value: Telephone lines are fixed telephone lines that connect a subscribers terminal equipment to the public switched telephone network and that have a port on a telephone exchange. Integrated services digital network channels and fixed wireless subscribers are included. State wise no of telephone lines has been furnished in the data.

field_group_name:name: Telecommunication
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Information and Communications
Telecom
title: State wise No of Telephone Lines

body:value: Telephone density or teledensity is the number of telephone connections for every hundred individuals living within an area. It varies widely across the nations and also between urban and rural areas within a country. Telephone density has significant correlation with the per capita GDP of the area.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Telecommunication
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Information and Communications
Telecom
title: Tele Density of India

body:value: Data contains Internet Subscribers, Average Revenue per User and Minutes of Use per User. Average revenue per user (sometimes known as average revenue per unit), usually abbreviated to ARPU, is a measure used primarily by consumer communications and networking companies, defined as the total revenue divided by the number of subscribers.

field_group_name:name: Telecommunication
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog

Telecom
title: Internet Subscribers, Average Revenue per User and Minutes of Use per User

field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Industries
Tourism
title: Tourism Revenue in India

body:value: The Ministry of Tourism designs national policies for the development and promotion of tourism. In the process, the Ministry consults and collaborates with other stakeholders in the sector including various Central Ministries/agencies, state governments, Union Territories and the representatives of the private sector. Concerted efforts are being made to promote new forms of tourism such as rural, cruise, medical and eco-tourism. India's rich history and its cultural and geographical diversity make its international tourism appeal large and diverse. It presents heritage and cultural tourism along with medical, business, educational and sports tourism. Get data on visitors arrival in India from different countries by Air, Sea and Land. Data also contains state wise number of visitor arrivals in India.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Tourism
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Industries
Tourism
title: Visitors Arrival in India

body:value: Outlay means the spending or disbursement of money. The Data contains State wise approved outlay with Development of major heads and Sub-heads like Agriculture and allied activities, Rural development, Special areas programmes, Irrigation and flood control, Energy, Industry and minerals, Transport, Science,technology and environment, General economic services, Social services, General services.

field_group_name:name: Financial Resources
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog

Revenue
title: Approved outlay

body:value: Committed expenditure is defined by the Comptroller and Auditor General as the expenditure on interest payments, salaries and wages, pensions and subsidies. The Data contains Actual, Pre-actual and Budgeted Expenditure for Committed expenditure, Population and per capita Committed expenditure of states & Uts.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Financial Resources
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: All
title: Committed expenditure of the states (Salaries+pensions+Interest payments)

body:value: Fiscal deficit/surplus is the difference between the governments expenditures and its revenues (excluding the money its borrowed). A countrys fiscal deficit is usually communicated as a percentage of its gross domestic product (GDP). The Data contains Actual, Pre-actual and Budgeted Expenditure for Fiscal deficit/surplus, GSDP and Fiscal deficit/surplus as percentage of GSDP of states & Uts.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Financial Resources
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Finance
Revenue
title: Fiscal deficit/surplus

body:value: It represents interest payable on any type of borrowings  bonds, loans, convertible debt or lines of credit. It is basically calculated as the interest rate times the outstanding principal amount of the debt. The Data contains Actual, Pre-actual and Budgeted Expenditure for Interest payment, total revenue reciepts(TRR) and Interest payment as percentage of TRR of states & Uts.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Financial Resources
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Finance
Revenue
title: Interest payment

body:value: Non-plan expenditure is accounted for by interest payments, subsidies (mainly on food and fertilisers), wage and salary payments to government employees, grants to States and Union Territories governments, pensions, police, economic services in various sectors, other general services such as tax collection, social services, grants to foreign governments, defence, loans to public enterprises, loans to States, Union Territories. The Data contains Actual, Pre-actual and Budgeted Expenditure for Non plan expenditure, GSDP and Non plan expenditure as percentageage of GSDP of states & Uts.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Financial Resources
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Finance
Revenue
title: Non plan expenditure

body:value: The amount of liability which is yet to be paid as on the balance sheet is known as outstanding liability. The Data contains Actual, Pre-actual and Budgeted Expenditure for State Governments Outstanding liabilities (public debt), Outstanding liabilities, GSDP and Outstanding liabilities as percentage to GSDP of states & Uts.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Financial Resources
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Finance
Revenue
title: Outstanding liabilities (public debt)

body:value: Health Ministry is regularly compiling and disseminating information on rural health care services through publication Rural Health Statistics in India. Data provides data of Rural Health Statistics 2012.  Rural Health Statistics 2012 contains Infrastructural information and building positions of Sub Centres, Primary Health Centres (PHCs) and Community Health Centres (CHCs). It also contains state wise number of Sub Divisional Hospital, District Hospital & Mobile Medical Units. Get state/Ut wise information of Specialist Doctors, Health Workers, Auxiliary Mid wife Nurse (ANM), Nursing Staffs, Health Assistants, Radiographers, Pharmacists. It includes information of staffs in position, requirement of staffs and shortfall. District wise availability of health centres has also been furnished in the data.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
State
field_group_name:name: Rural Health Statistics
field_ministry_department:name: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
Department of Health and Family Welfare
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
title: Rural Health Statistics in India - 2012

field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India

body:value: Net attendance ratio is defined as the ratio of the number of persons in the official age-group attending any educational institution in a particular class-group to the total number persons in the age-group. Data is disaggregated by Social Groups.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: India Human Development Report
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Social Development
title: Net attendance ratio

body:value: The average annual expenditure incurred per student of age 5-29 years currently pursuing general education has been furnished in the Data. It is disaggregated by Social Groups and major religion communities.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: India Human Development Report
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Social Development
title: Average annual expenditure per student of age 529 years in general education

body:value: literacy rate defined as percentage of persons aged 7 years and above who can read and write a short simple sentence with understanding for a given time period. Data is disaggregated by Social Groups and major religion communities.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: India Human Development Report
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Social Development
title: Literacy Rate by Social Groups and Major Religious Communities

body:value: Percentage of households that have electricity facility for domestic use. Data is expressed as per cent. Data is disaggregated by location.  Domestic use of the electricity may be for lighting or cooking or for both. Electricity may be used legally or illegally and the electricity may be supplied to the household either through public agencies, corporations or by private suppliers. However, if the household makes its own arrangement, either through generator or solar panel, to generate electricity, the household will not be considered as having electricity for domestic use. Data is disaggregated by Social Groups and major religion communities.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: India Human Development Report
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Social Development
title: Distribution of households with electricity for domestic use

body:value: Child labour refers to the employment of children in any work that deprives children of their childhood, interferes with their ability to attend regular school, and that is mentally, physically, socially or morally dangerous and harmful. Data contains the percentage of de jure (According to the Law)  children age 5-14 years, who were engaged in household chores, family work or in paid/unpaid work for an someone who is not the member of the household. Data is disaggregated by Gender, Social Groups and major religion communities.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: India Human Development Report
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Social Development
title: Child Labour (age 5 to 14)

body:value: The place of birth (POB) is the place where a person was born. This place is often used in legal documents, together with name and date of birth, to uniquely identify a person. Percentage of live births disaggregated by population with No Education and population with more than 12 years of education.  Live birth refers to the complete expulsion or extraction from its mother of a product of conception, irrespective of the duration of the pregnancy, which, after such separation, breathes or shows any other evidence of life - e.g. beating of the heart, pulsation of the umbilical cord or definite movement of voluntary muscles - whether or not the umbilical cord has been cut or the placenta is attached. Each product of such a birth is considered live born.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: India Human Development Report

body:value: Children out of school are the number of primary-school-age children not enrolled in primary or secondary school. The data desaggregated by social groups (Scheduled caste, Scheduled Tribe, Other Backward Class) and major religious communities (Hindu, Muslim, Sikh, Christian).

title: Out of school children (6 to 17 years)

body:value:
National Employment Service or Employment Exchange, operated by the Directorate General of Employment and Training, Ministry of Labour, runs over 900 Employment Exchanges in order to bring about a better matching of the demand for, and the supply of work opportunities. Job seekers register themselves with these Employment Exchanges and get notified as soon as any vacancy in the Government sector matches their desired profile.
According to the Employment Exchanges (Compulsory Notification of Vacancies) Act, 1959 in any State or area thereof, the employer in every establishment in public sector in that State or area shall, before filling up any vacancy in any employment in that establishment, notify that vacancy to such employment exchanges as may be prescribed.
Employment Exchanges play a significant role in assisting the youth in finding employment in paid jobs. They also assist them in starting Self-Employment Ventures through Vocational Guidance activities. Registering the applications of job-seekers and notifying them about vacancies, collection and dissemination of Employment Market Information, Vocational Guidance to students and the youth are the major functions of Employment Exchanges.
The data contains information like number of employment exchanges, new registrations,  live registrations, vacancies notified, placements effected etc.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India

field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog

title: Employment exchanges Data
